001/*

003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.toml;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
027
028/**
029 * Parses TOML v1.0.0 text into POJOs.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Handles <c>Content-Type</c>: <bc>application/toml</bc>
033 *
034 * <h5 class='topic'>Limitations compared to JSON</h5>
035 * <p>
036 * TOML is a configuration format with a more restricted type system than JSON.
037 * The following features supported by {@link JsonParser}
038 * are not available or limited in TOML:
039 * </p>
040 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
041 *    <li><b>Null values</b> — TOML has no null type. Absent keys yield null for object properties.
042 *       The parser treats the configured <c>nullValue</c> string (e.g. <c>&lt;NULL&gt;</c>) as Java null
043 *       when it appears as a value.
044 *    <li><b>Non-string map keys</b> — TOML keys are always strings. Non-string key types
045 *       (e.g. <c>Map&lt;Integer,String&gt;</c>) are converted via {@link ParserSession#convertAttrToType(Object, String, org.apache.juneau.marshall.ClassMeta) convertAttrToType}.
046 *       Null keys are serialized as the string <c>null</c> and converted back to Java null during parsing.
047 *    <li><b>Polymorphic types</b> — Parsing to interfaces or abstract classes requires a
048 *       {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContext.Builder#beanDictionary(Class[]) bean dictionary}
049 *       when no <c>_type</c> discriminator is present in the document.
050 *    <li><b>Duration</b> — TOML has no native duration type. Quoted ISO-8601 duration strings
051 *       (e.g. <c>"PT1H30M"</c>) are parsed to <c>Duration</c> via {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.utils.Iso8601Utils}.
052 *    <li><b>Year and YearMonth</b> — Bare <c>Year</c> (4-digit integer) parses as <c>Long</c>, not <c>Year</c>.
053 *       Use quoted strings (e.g. <c>"2024"</c>) or <c>yyyy-MM</c> for <c>YearMonth</c>; swaps may be needed
054 *       for full compatibility.
055 * </ul>
056 *
057 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
058 * <p class='bjava'>
059 *    <jc>// Parse TOML into a JsonMap</jc>
060 *    String <jv>toml</jv> = <js>"name = \"myapp\"\nport = 8080"</js>;
061 *    JsonMap <jv>parsed</jv> = TomlParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.parse(<jv>toml</jv>, JsonMap.<jk>class</jk>);
062 *
063 *    <jc>// Parse into a bean</jc>
064 *    MyConfig <jv>config</jv> = TomlParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.parse(<jv>toml</jv>, MyConfig.<jk>class</jk>);
065 *
066 *    <jc>// Parse into a parameterized type (e.g. Map with type args)</jc>
067 *    Map&lt;String, Object&gt; <jv>map</jv> = TomlParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.parse(<jv>toml</jv>, Map.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, Object.<jk>class</jk>);
068 *
069 *    <jc>// Use the marshaller for convenience</jc>
070 *    <jv>parsed</jv> = Toml.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>toml</jv>, JsonMap.<jk>class</jk>);
071 * </p>
072 *
073 * <h5 class='figure'>Example input (Map of name/age):</h5>
074 * <p class='bcode'>
075 *    name = "Alice"
076 *    age = 30
077 * </p>
078 *
079 * <h5 class='figure'>Complex (nested table + array):</h5>
080 * <p class='bcode'>
081 *    name = "Alice"
082 *    age = 30
083 *    tags = ["a", "b", "c"]
084 *
085 *    [address]
086 *    street = "123 Main St"
087 *    city = "Boston"
088 *    state = "MA"
089 * </p>
090 *
091 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
092 *    <li class='link'><a href="https://toml.io/en/v1.0.0">TOML v1.0.0 Specification</a>
093 * </ul>
094 */
095@SuppressWarnings({
096   "java:S110", // Builder pattern requires many parameters
097   "java:S115",  // ARG_ prefix follows framework convention
098   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's parser session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
099})
100public class TomlParser extends ReaderParser implements RecordReadable {
101
102   private static final String ARG_copyFrom = "copyFrom";
103
104   private static final String PROP_nullValue = "nullValue";
105
106   /**
107    * Builder for {@link TomlParser}.
108    */
109   public static class Builder extends ReaderParser.Builder<Builder> {
110
111      private static final Cache<HashKey,TomlParser> CACHE = Cache.of(HashKey.class, TomlParser.class).build();
112
113      private String nullValue = "<NULL>";
114
115      protected Builder() {
116         consumes("application/toml");
117      }
118
119      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
120         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
121         nullValue = copyFrom.nullValue;
122      }
123
124      protected Builder(TomlParser copyFrom) {
125         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
126         nullValue = copyFrom.nullValue;
127      }
128
129      /**
130       * String that denotes null when parsing. Must match the serializer's nullValue.
131       *
132       * @param value The null marker string.
133       * @return This object.
134       */
135      public Builder nullValue(String value) {
136         nullValue = value;
137         return this;
138      }
139
140      @Override
141      public TomlParser build() {
142         return cache(CACHE).build(TomlParser.class);
143      }
144
145      @Override
146      public Builder copy() {
147         return new Builder(this);
148      }
149
150      @Override
151      public HashKey hashKey() {
152         return HashKey.of(super.hashKey(), nullValue);
153      }
154   }
155
156   /** Default parser instance. */
157   public static final TomlParser DEFAULT = new TomlParser(create());
158
159   /**
160    * Creates a new builder.
161    *
162    * @return A new builder.
163    */
164   public static Builder create() {
165      return new Builder();
166   }
167
168   protected final String nullValue;
169
170   /**
171    * Constructor.
172    *
173    * @param builder The builder.
174    */
175   public TomlParser(Builder builder) {
176      super(builder);
177      nullValue = builder.nullValue != null ? builder.nullValue : "<NULL>";
178   }
179
180   /**
181    * Returns the string treated as null when parsing.
182    *
183    * @return The null marker.
184    */
185   public String getNullValue() {
186      return nullValue;
187   }
188
189   @Override
190   public TomlParserSession.Builder createSession() {
191      return TomlParserSession.create(this);
192   }
193
194   @Override
195   public Builder copy() {
196      return new Builder(this);
197   }
198
199   @Override
200   protected FluentMap<String, Object> properties() {
201      return super.properties().a(PROP_nullValue, nullValue);
202   }
203
204   /**
205    * Convenience delegator that opens a {@link RecordReader} over the input using
206    * <b>default session arguments</b> (mirrors {@link #parse(Object, Class)}).
207    *
208    * <p>
209    * The real implementation lives on {@link TomlParserSession#parseRecords(Object)}.  Callers
210    * that need request-derived configuration (locale, timezone, schema, swaps) should call
211    * {@link #createSession()} and invoke {@link TomlParserSession#parseRecords(Object)} on the
212    * built session instead.
213    *
214    * @param input The input.
215    * @return A new {@link RecordReader} cursor.
216    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
217    */
218   @Override /* RecordReadable */
219   public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
220      return ((RecordReadable) getSession()).parseRecords(input);
221   }
222
223   @Override /* RecordReadable */
224   public boolean isRecordStreaming() {
225      return false;
226   }
227}
